steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Recall the area formula for a square
steps.content: A square with side length s has area = s × s = s².

steps.title: Substitute the given side length
steps.content: The side length is 94 cm, so: Area = 94 × 94 cm²

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• $2·94$ $2·94$ — this is a fraction of the perimeter (2 sides), not area. Eliminated.
  • $2·94·94$ $2·94·94$ — doubles the area; no geometric basis for the factor of 2. Eliminated.
  • $4·94$ $4·94$ — this is the perimeter (4 sides), not area. Eliminated.
  • $94·94$ $94·94$ — side times side, which is the correct area formula. That is the correct answer.
